solve_notes: Added a trailing-dot check in the `default` case of `normalizeRelativePath` to throw `CorruptedPathDetected` when any path segment ends with a dot. Also updated the test file to remove the now-invalid `dirname./` success case and add trailing-dot paths to the corruption data provider.
task_text:
Fix WhitespacePathNormalizer not rejecting paths with trailing dots on Windows

In `src/WhitespacePathNormalizer.php`, `normalizePath` strips
directory traversal (`..`) and empty segments but does not reject
paths with trailing dots (e.g., `"file."`). On Windows, trailing
dots are silently stripped by the OS, causing `file.` to resolve
to `file`, leading to unintended overwrites. Fix `normalizePath`
to reject paths containing segments with trailing dots by throwing
`CorruptedPathDetected`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: dpFunkyWhitespacePaths
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: knowing the existing test data provider structure prevents the agent from creating a separate test method instead of extending the correct one

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the edited method and the exception factory. An AI agent would additionally look at rejectFunkyWhiteSpace to understand how existing rejection is patterned, and at dpFunkyWhitespacePaths to know how to extend the test data — without seeing these, the agent might duplicate the rejection pattern incorrectly or add a standalone test method instead of appending to the existing provider.
